name: Kaleji Fry (Liver)
tagline: Goat liver pan-fry — densest source of iron, B12, copper, and vitamin A

hero.alt: Dark-brown liver pieces stir-fried with onions and ginger juliennes in a kadhai
hero.caption: kaleji fry — quick-seared with onion and chili

steps.title: Clean and rinse the liver
steps.detail: Rinse cubed liver thoroughly in salted water for 5 minutes, then pat dry. This removes the blood and most of the metallic edge.
steps.tip: Soak in a tablespoon of vinegar for 5 min if you want zero metallic taste.

steps.title: Sauté the masala
steps.detail: Heat mustard oil till it smokes. Brown the onions till just golden, add ginger-garlic and green chili, sauté 1 minute. Stir in chili powder and pepper.
steps.tip: Mustard oil pairs with liver's iron-rich profile — don't substitute.

steps.title: Quick-sear the liver
steps.detail: Crank the heat. Add the liver and stir-fry hard for 4–5 minutes till just cooked through but still pink in the centre. Add garam masala and salt at the end. Squeeze lemon, serve immediately.
steps.tip: Liver gets rubbery the moment it's overcooked — pull at the first sign of firmness.

healthFacts: Goat liver: 50µg vitamin A and 60µg B12 per 100g — multiples of daily need in one serving.
Heme iron at 6mg per 100g — best food source for restoring ferritin.
Copper, folate, and selenium content all hit clinical-supplement levels.
Limit to 1–2 servings/week — vitamin A is fat-soluble and accumulates.
